channel.title: About on Vishpala
channel.description: Recent content in About on Vishpala

item.title: Accessibility Statement
item.description:
Commitment

Vishpala is committed to ensuring that this website is accessible to people with disabilities. We target conformance with the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.2 at Level AA.

Accessibility is a core discipline of our studio. We do not treat it as a compliance exercise.

Current status

This site has been designed and built with accessibility as a primary requirement. The following measures are in place:

  • Semantic HTML structure with appropriate landmark regions
  • Skip navigation link to bypass the site header
  • All images have meaningful alternative text or are marked as decorative
  • Colour contrast meets WCAG 2.2 AA requirements for all text
  • The site is fully navigable by keyboard
  • Focus indicators are visible and meet contrast requirements
  • The site has been tested with VoiceOver (macOS and iOS) and NVDA (Windows)
  • Language is declared in the HTML for both English and French content
  • All interactive elements have accessible names

Known limitations

This site is under active development. We will document any known accessibility issues here as they are identified.

item.title: Legal
item.description:
Licence

Unless otherwise noted, the content of this site — text, guides, and documentation — is published under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International Licence (CC BY-SA 4.0).

You are free to share and adapt this material for any purpose, including commercial use, provided you give appropriate credit and distribute any adaptations under the same licence.

Code samples published on this site are released under the MIT Licence unless otherwise noted.

Terms of use

This site is provided for informational purposes. The content here represents our current thinking and practice; it does not constitute legal, technical, or professional advice for your specific situation.

item.title: Privacy by Design
item.description:
Principle

Privacy at Vishpala is not a legal obligation we satisfy at minimum cost. It is a foundational design principle that shapes every decision about what we build, what data we collect, and what infrastructure we use.

We apply the seven foundational principles of Privacy by Design as articulated by Ann Cavoukian:

  1. Proactive, not reactive — preventive, not remedial
  2. Privacy as the default setting
  3. Privacy embedded into design
  4. Full functionality — positive-sum, not zero-sum
  5. End-to-end security — full lifecycle protection
  6. Visibility and transparency
  7. Respect for user privacy — keep it user-centric

What we collect and why

This website — we do not use analytics tracking. We do not set cookies. We do not load third-party scripts. No data about visitors to this site is collected, transmitted to third parties, or retained.

item.title: Sustainability Commitment
item.description:
Principle

We build things that are meant to last. That commitment extends beyond the technical quality of the work to its environmental and operational footprint.

Sustainability at Vishpala means building systems that consume the minimum necessary resources, that can be maintained by small teams without specialised infrastructure, and that do not accumulate complexity faster than the capacity to understand and operate them.

Digital sustainability

Efficient infrastructure — we design systems sized to actual workload. We do not recommend infrastructure at a scale that requires constant management overhead to justify its cost or complexity. Smaller, simpler, better-understood systems are almost always more sustainable than large, complex ones.

item.title: About Vishpala
item.description:
Who we are

Vishpala is a multidisciplinary studio. We are technologists, designers, and infrastructure specialists who work together on problems that require all three disciplines to solve well.

We take our name from the Rigvedic figure Vishpala — a warrior who loses a leg in battle and is given an iron prosthetic by the Ashvins so she can return to the field. We are interested in the idea of prosthetics: tools and infrastructure that extend capability, restore agency, and allow people to do what they came to do.
